summary:
Cocaine toxicity appears highly plausible based on a range of symptoms including visual disturbances, chest discomfort, heart rhythm issues; these are consistent findings associated with acute cocaine exposure affecting both central nervous and circulatory systems. Given this multi-system impact typical for severe drug intoxication scenarios, further toxicology screening should prioritize detecting cocaine levels. \boxed{Cocaine Toxicity}

The summary focuses only on critical aspects directly linked to diagnosing the condition from provided information while adhering strictly within two concise statements. It highlights how diverse symptomatology can converge into one diagnostic conclusion supported through medical knowledge bases like UMLS used here effectively.
